note: design↔live element correspondence for the app rail + its Run count badge. DESIGN = the vendored v3 app harness (design-kit/app-v3/index.html, rail transcribed verbatim from copilot-app.jsx:295-306, styled by copilot.css:61-75) at ?dest=chats (badge) and ?dest=workspace (nobadge). LIVE = the REAL ChatShell→AppRail (packages/chat-surface/src/shell/AppRail.tsx) rendered by lib/render-live-rail-badge.test.tsx in the SOLO 6-destination profile with railBadges={{run:1}} and railIdentity={{initial:'S'}}. The live rail is styled almost entirely by INLINE style objects in AppRail.tsx (railStyle/itemsStyle/footStyle/railButtonStyle/badgeStyle/avatarStyle/activeBarStyle) — there is no rail stylesheet — so every root cause below is a literal in that file or a design-system token it references. Live anchors therefore key off the rail's stable data-* attributes, not class names.

elements.label: shell.body.grid
elements.expectDivergence: the design's .mw-body is `grid-template-columns:auto 1fr` (copilot.css:61) with the rail sizing itself; the live shell declares the rail column EXPLICITLY as `${APP_RAIL_WIDTH}px …` (ChatShell.tsx:250-252) so the grid and the rail share one exported constant (AppRail.tsx:14 RAIL_WIDTH=48, re-exported line 314). Same resolved 48px column, different authoring strategy — intentional single-source-of-truth, not drift. The live shell also carries the context/right-rail columns the mock's simplified frame has no equivalent for.

elements.label: rail.item.run.label
elements.expectDivergence: the design ships a `.rl` text span that is `display:none` (copilot.css:69) — zero visual footprint, present only as dead markup from an earlier labelled-rail iteration. The live rail carries the same affordance on the button itself via `title` + `aria-label` (AppRail.tsx:262-263), which is strictly better for assistive tech. Absence is intentional and unmeasurable; INFO, not drift.

elements.label: rail.item.active.bar
elements.expectDivergence: the design draws the 2px accent bar as a CSS ::before pseudo-element (copilot.css:72) which querySelector cannot address; the live rail draws the identical box as a real inset <span data-rail-active-bar> (AppRail.tsx:139-150, 253-259) so it can be measured and tested. The design side is therefore unmatched BY CONSTRUCTION — this anchor exists to prove the live bar's geometry, not to claim the design lacks one.
